summary.intent: Run QA validation on the CHA-16 feature branch implementing crm.contacts.* RPC methods, verify all tests pass, and ensure auth scope tests cover the new endpoints.
summary.outcome: All 26 CRM tests passed, clippy and format checks were clean, and missing auth tests for crm.contacts.getByExternal and crm.contacts.getWithChannels were identified and added. Changes were committed and pushed.

repo: The codebase pattern (following cron, hooks, identity modules) requires explicit auth scope tests for each new RPC method to verify read scope gates access.
Auth tests for RPC methods live in crates/gateway/src/methods/mod.rs.
The project uses clippy and rustfmt as mandatory quality gates before committing.

code.finding: Auth scope tests for new RPC methods must be added here following the established pattern used by cron, hooks, and identity methods. The new crm.contacts.getByExternal and crm.contacts.getWithChannels methods were missing these tests initially.

workflow: QA skill runs a preamble to check branch info and working tree cleanliness before executing any validation.
The workflow detects the base branch and diffs against main to scope what needs to be validated.
Auth coverage gaps are treated as blocking issues that must be fixed before the QA run can complete successfully.

friction: Auth tests for crm.contacts.getByExternal and crm.contacts.getWithChannels were missing from the initial implementation — caught only during QA, requiring a fix commit on the second run.
